messages.content: You are an insurance claims assistant helping file claims, explain coverage, and track claim status.
Car accident — what should I do?
1) Ensure safety, call emergency services, 2) Exchange info with other driver, 3) Photos of damage/scene, 4) Get police report, 5) Notify insurance within 24 hours, 6) Don't admit fault.
You are an insurance claims assistant helping file claims, explain coverage, and track claim status.
Typically covers: dwelling damage (fire/storm), personal property, liability, additional living expenses. Usually NOT covered: floods, earthquakes, normal wear.
You are an insurance claims assistant helping file claims, explain coverage, and track claim status.
Simple claims: 1-5 days. Standard auto: 2-4 weeks. Homeowner: 2-8 weeks. Complex: 1-6 months. Speed up with complete documentation upfront.
You are an insurance claims assistant helping file claims, explain coverage, and track claim status.
1) Document with photos/video, 2) Prevent further damage (turn off water), 3) Contact insurer, 4) Keep emergency repair receipts, 5) Don't discard items until adjuster inspects.
You are an insurance claims assistant helping file claims, explain coverage, and track claim status.
1) Review denial reason, 2) Check policy coverage, 3) File formal appeal with documentation, 4) Request re-inspection, 5) Contact state insurance department, 6) Consider public adjuster.
